description:
India Book (IB) II, 3: Letter from Amram b. Yosef to Nahray b. Nissim inquiring about a letter sent to Aden Alexandria. Dating: 1094–97.
The first 27 lines consist mainly of greetings and expressions of admiration for Nahray and Mevorākh, the Rayyis. Amram then asks if there is any news of his brother-in-law Nissīm's arrival.
ʿAmram has suffered from ophthalmia for one year and cannot find anyone to cure it. He excuses himself from coming in person to pay his respects to the Nagid and asks Nahray to represent him. Information by ASE and from Goitein and Friedman, India Traders

See IB II, 2–9: On Abu ’l-Faraj Nissīm, who disappeared in India, with his camphor (Goitein and Friedman, India Traders, 288–290)
tags: India, illness letter 969-1517, ib-untranslated-eng, Illness, IB2
description_he: ספר הודו II,3: מכתב מעמרם בן יוסף לנהראי בן ניסים על מכתב לעדן, מחלת עיניים ובקשות, 1094-97
